D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Hurter & Driffield: Density vs Log-Exposure

colour negative film draws a non-linear D-logE curve per layer; toe, straight portion (γ), and shoulder produce the film "look"

The Hurter & Driffield (1890) curve plots developed density (D = log₁₀ opacity) against log exposure. The toe rolls underexposure into deep shadows; the straight portion has slope γ (gamma) — the working dynamic range; the shoulder rolls highlights into the maximum density. Colour negative film has three such curves, one per dye layer.

Drag γ to tilt the straight-portion slope — high γ is hard contrasty; low γ is soft. Compare colour negative (long toe, soft shoulder — preserves highlights) with positive slide film (steep γ, short toe — limited latitude but vivid).
